en.d: suffering from xenophobia, or the fear of outsiders
en.e: Those remarks make it pretty clear how xenophobic you are.

en.d: of the color intermediate between green and orange in the color spectrum
en.e: The new rug you bought is fairly xanthous, don't you think?

en.d: the 24th letter of the Roman alphabet
en.e: The farmer signed with an x.

vi: "Xylophone" bắt đầu bằng con chữ X

en.d: the 14th letter of the Greek alphabet

vi: (viết tắt) của double-cross (từ lóng) sự lừa dối; sự phản bội

en.e: It's my xcvii birthday, Augustus!
The Sahara desert is a xeric environment – it rarely rains there.
Cacti are xerophytic plants.

en.d: denoting or relating to cells or tissues from individuals belonging to different species
en.e: The xenogenic reactions of trees and mosses is quite fascinating.

en.d: relating to genes or characteristics or conditions carried on the X chromosome
en.e: I think it might be an X-linked mutation.

en.d: of or relating to copying paper or other items, aka xerography
en.e: Our xerographic machine was on the fritz.

en.d: obtaining images by the use of X rays
en.e: The pictures from the X-raying were analyzed by the dentist.

en.d: forming an image by the action of light on a specially coated charged plate
en.e: The machine used xerography to print copies.

en.d: radiography using X-rays and xerographic techniques
en.e: The radiologist specialized in xeroradiography.

en.d: a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species
a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species
en.e: The comparable sizes of organs in pigs and humans means that xenotransplantation could be feasible.
I cultured a flask full of xanthomonad.

en.d: a percussion instrument with wooden bars tuned to produce a chromatic scale, with resonators to help with the sound quality
en.e: The children were encouraged to play the xylophone.

en.d: take an x-ray of something or somebody
en.e: The doctor x-rayed my chest

vi: (thường số nhiều) tia X, X quang

en.d: a copy made by a xerographic printer
tissue from an animal of one species used as a temporary graft, as in cases of severe burns, on an individual of another species
en.e: The surgeons used skin from a pig as a xenograft.

en.d: the horizontal axis in a plane coordinate system
en.e: We marked the x-axis with the number of visitors that the museum received.

en.d: a piece of rock of different origin from the igneous rock in which it is embedded

en.e: The xylophonist played the Danse Macabre.

en.d: the production of offspring that is distinct from either parent
en.e: The two creatures did not look alike, but were thought to be different stages in xenogenesis.

en.d: plant adapted for life with a limited supply of water
en.e: The gardener loved the easy maintenance of the xerophyte.

vi: (thực vật học) cây ưa khô, cây chịu hạn

en.d: plant adapted for life with a limited supply of water
en.e: Cacti are some of the most well-known xerophiles.

en.d: the woody part of plants: the supporting and water-conducting tissue, consisting primarily of tracheids and vessels
en.e: The xylem carries water up into the tree.

en.d: a skin problem marked by the development of irregular yellow nodules on the eyelids, neck and back
en.e: The patient is suffering from xanthoma.

en.d: widespread xanthomas (especially on elbows and knees)
an abnormal yellow discoloration of the skin
en.e: The child was so unhealthy that the skin displayed xanthosis.

en.d: a fear of foreigners or strangers
en.e: I think your xenophobia might be cured by a trip abroad.

en.d: visual defect in which objects appear to have a yellowish hue
en.e: The drug digitalin is thought to cause xanthopsia.

en.d: the condition of not containing or being covered by a liquid, especially water
en.e: The xerotes of the skin was helped by applying a wax.

en.d: a mild form of ichthyosis characterized by abnormal dryness and roughness of the skin
a mild form of ichthyosis characterized by abnormal dryness and roughness of the skin
abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes
abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes
abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes
en.e: Your eyes have a condition called xeroma.

en.d: abnormal dryness of the mouth resulting from decreased secretion of saliva
excess carotene in the blood stream
en.e: Eating too many carrots had contributed to the child's xanthemia.

en.d: a colorless, odorless, inert gaseous element occurring in the earth's atmosphere in trace amounts
en.e: The canister was filled with xenon.

en.d: yellow carotenoid pigments in plants and animal fats and egg yolks
yellow carotenoid pigments in plants and animal fats and egg yolks
a salt or ester of xanthic acid
crystalline oxidation product of the metabolism of nucleoproteins
en.e: Once generated from guanine, xanthine is often turned into uric acid.

en.d: a sugar extracted from wood or straw
en.e: Xylose is found in most plants that humans consume.

en.d: a brown-to-yellow mineral that is a phosphate of yttrium in crystalline form
a colorless flammable volatile liquid hydrocarbon used as a solvent
